content.text:
Information Systems Security
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEM1  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
This module covers the essential aspects of information systems security, from theory to practical and organisational aspects, as well as security management.
Recommended prior knowledge:
Information systems, networks, basic mathematics.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to Information Systems Security
Definitions and key concepts: information security, cybersecurity, ISS (Information Systems Security)
Security pillars: confidentiality, integrity, availability, authenticity, non-repudiation
Threats and attacks: malware, network attacks, social engineering, insider threats
ISS challenges for companies and organisations
Security Principles and Techniques
Access control: authentication, authorisation, identity management, RBAC
Cryptography: symmetric and asymmetric encryption, hash functions, digital signatures, digital certificates
Network security: firewalls, VPN, IDS/IPS, secure protocols (SSL/TLS, SSH)
Application security: secure development, vulnerability analysis, penetration testing
Physical and logical security
Security Management and Compliance
Information security management systems (ISMS)
Risk assessment and risk management
Security policies and procedures
Crisis management and business continuity plan
Regulatory compliance: GDPR, ISO/IEC 27001 and 27002, etc.
Security audits
Specific Security Topics
Cloud security
IoT (Internet of Things) security
Database security
Operating system security
Security and artificial intelligence
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): written tests, personal work, tests
Practical work includes:
Analysis of security breach situations in information systems; simulation of various IS security techniques.
References
Ludovic Mé, Yves Deswarte (2006).
Sécurité des systèmes d'information
. Edition Hermès-Lavoisier.
Donald L Pipkin (2000).
Sécurité des systèmes d'information
. Pearson Education.
ISO/IEC Standards 27001 and 27002. www.iso.org/standard/75652.html
J. M. Stewart, M. Chapple, D. Gibson (2012).
CISSP: Certified Information Systems Security Professional, Study Guide
. John Wiley & Sons.
M.Bennasar, A.Champenois, P. Arnould et T.Rivat (2007).
Manager la sécurité du SI
. DUNOD Editions.
